# Sisal Full LLM Context

> Generated by `deno task docs:llms` from Sisal source documentation, package manifests, and `deno doc --json` output for public exports.

## Compact Index

# Sisal

> Sisal is a Deno-first, JSR-native database toolkit for typed schemas, safe SQL, query builders, migration planning, and small adapter packages.

Workspace package version: 0.4.0. The core ORM is driverless; adapters for PostgreSQL, Neon, SQLite, and libSQL/Turso live at explicit package boundaries.

Use `llms-full.txt` when you need a single file containing the API index, package exports, and the source documentation.

## Primary Documentation

- [Full LLM Context](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/llms-full.txt): Generated combined context from Sisal docs, package manifests, and exported API docs.
- [API Reference](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/api.html): Complete reference for the public API of every Sisal package, generated from a full read of the source. Sisal is a Deno-first database toolkit, published to JSR, made of six packages with strict boundaries:
- [Migration Notes](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/migration-notes.html): Sisal replaces the earlier ORM and migration package direction with a focused database toolkit.
- [Drizzle Parity](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/drizzle-parity.html): This is the guiding document for how Sisal's API evolves. It maps every relevant part of the Drizzle ORM 0.45.2 surface to Sisal, records where we intentionally diverge, and lays out the roadmap to close the gaps...
- [PostgreSQL compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/pg-compatibility.html): Sisal's PostgreSQL adapter (@sisal/pg) is verified end-to-end against a real server on every supported major version. The suite connects with the bundled jsr:@db/postgres@0.19.5 driver, applies generated DDL, and...
- [Neon compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/neon-compatibility.html): Sisal's Neon adapter (@sisal/neon) targets Neon serverless PostgreSQL. It speaks the Postgres wire protocol over a WebSocket via jsr:@neon/serverless and **reuses the Postgres SQL dialect and DDL**, so its feature...
- [SQLite compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/sqlite-compatibility.html): Sisal's SQLite adapter (@sisal/sqlite) is verified end-to-end against a real, embedded SQLite database. The suite opens a temp file with the bundled jsr:@db/sqlite driver, applies generated DDL, and exercises every...
- [libSQL / Turso compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/libsql-compatibility.html): Sisal's libSQL adapter (@sisal/libsql) is verified end-to-end against a real libSQL database. libSQL is a SQLite fork, so the SQL surface matches SQLite; what differs is the **connection** — a local file:, an...
- [Benchmarks](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/benchmarks.html): Sisal benchmarks measure the work the **library** does — building SQL, rendering it, and turning driver rows into typed results — in isolation from the database and the network. A query's wall-clock time is dominated...
- [Security](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/security.html): This is the single source of truth for Sisal's security posture: a **code-level audit**, a **living roadmap** of addressed and open concerns, and the **invariants pinned by tests** so the posture cannot silently...
- [Package Architecture (long-term)](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/architecture.html): Sisal is, and will remain, a **Deno-first, SQL-first, type-safe** toolkit for **relational** data access. It is not becoming an object-first ORM. As the surface grows from OLTP query building toward ETL, analytics,...
- [Editor LSP Setup](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/editor-lsp.html): Sisal is a Deno workspace, so editors should attach TypeScript files to deno lsp, not to a plain Node/TypeScript language server. A generic LSP client is fine as long as the server process is Deno's language server.
- [Feature matrix](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/feature-matrix.html): Source of truth: tools/feature_matrix.ts Regenerate: deno task docs:matrix · Verify: deno task docs:matrix:check -->
- [Roadmap Overview](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/roadmap.html): Sisal is a **Deno-first, SQL-first, type-safe** ORM and database toolkit for **relational** data across PostgreSQL, Neon, SQLite, and libSQL. It will not become an object-first ORM. This page is the index and...
- [v0.10.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.10.0-roadmap.html): The investigations (v0.6) and substrate (v0.8, v0.9) are done; v0.10 ships the **first preview of Sisal ETL** as a separate package. The model is deliberately small: a **job definition + a single-run runner** that...
- [v0.11.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.11.0-roadmap.html): With the analytical IR designed (v0.7) and the core IR stabilized (v0.8), v0.11 ships the first **Postgres-first analytical query layer** — a typed OLAP API that queries rollups and analytical tables using Sisal...
- [v0.12.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.12.0-roadmap.html): v0.12 introduces a **generic dashboard mapping layer** that turns analytics result sets into **semantic presentation models**. It is the last layer of the stack and the most easily misunderstood, so the boundary is...
- [v0.13+ Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.13.0-roadmap.html): DuckDB is attractive — but only **after** the analytical IR exists. v0.13+ is an **investigation** of DuckDB (and other OLAP engines) as a later **compiler / execution target** for the analytics IR built in...
- [v0.14+ Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.14.0-roadmap.html): The last milestone on the line, and the most conditional. v0.14+ investigates native/Rust acceleration **only if** benchmarks prove a real bottleneck that **neither SQL pushdown nor DuckDB** (v0.13+) can solve...
- [v0.4.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.4.0-roadmap.html): This document captures the API gaps surfaced while building the examples/neon-hot-feed example — a Reddit-style hot feed on Neon/PostgreSQL that must stay Deno Deploy / Neon-HTTP friendly. Each gap forced the example...
- [v0.5.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.5.0-roadmap.html): Where v0.4.0 closed _feature_ gaps surfaced by the neon-hot-feed example, v0.5.0 closes _parity_ gaps **between the adapters**.
- [v0.6.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.6.0-roadmap.html): Where v0.5.0 makes the OLTP builder boringly correct across the four dialects, v0.6.0 is the **readiness** milestone: it earns the right to build later packages (and reach a fifth dialect) by proving the foundation...
- [v0.7.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.7.0-roadmap.html): v0.7 carries **two parallel workstreams**:
- [v0.8.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.8.0-roadmap.html): v0.6 and v0.7 are investigations; v0.8 is where their findings become a **stable substrate**. The goal is to stabilize Sisal's internal SQL representation and expression system — and extract **@sisal/core** — so that...
- [v0.9.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.9.0-roadmap.html): With the IR stabilized in v0.8 and **MySQL added in v0.7**, v0.9 hardens the **five** adapters against the ETL/analytics SQL shapes surfaced by v0.6/v0.7, and turns the implicit, scattered dialect knowledge into an...

## Package APIs

- [@sisal/orm](https://jsr.io/@sisal/orm): Driverless schema, typed SQL, and ORM primitives for Sisal. Exports: ., ./core, ./error, ./logger, ./schema.
- [@sisal/migrate](https://jsr.io/@sisal/migrate): Migration planning, diffing, and running primitives for Sisal. Exports: ., ./cli, ./core, ./workflow.
- [@sisal/pg](https://jsr.io/@sisal/pg): PostgreSQL adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.
- [@sisal/sqlite](https://jsr.io/@sisal/sqlite): SQLite adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.
- [@sisal/libsql](https://jsr.io/@sisal/libsql): libSQL and Turso adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.
- [@sisal/neon](https://jsr.io/@sisal/neon): Neon serverless PostgreSQL adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.

## Project Context

- [Sisal](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal/blob/main/README.md): Sisal is a Deno-first database toolkit, published to JSR, for typed schemas, safe SQL builders, schema snapshots, migration planning, and explicit database adapters.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): This is the canonical changelog for Sisal workspace releases.
- [Repository](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal): Source code, issues, examples, workflows, and package directories.

## Package Manifests

### @sisal/orm 0.4.0

Driverless schema, typed SQL, and ORM primitives for Sisal.

Package path: `./packages/orm`

Exports:
- `.` -> `./mod.ts`
- `./core` -> `./core/mod.ts`
- `./error` -> `./error.ts`
- `./logger` -> `./logger.ts`
- `./schema` -> `./schema.ts`

### @sisal/migrate 0.4.0

Migration planning, diffing, and running primitives for Sisal.

Package path: `./packages/migrate`

Exports:
- `.` -> `./mod.ts`
- `./cli` -> `./cli.ts`
- `./core` -> `./core/mod.ts`
- `./workflow` -> `./core/workflow.ts`

### @sisal/pg 0.4.0

PostgreSQL adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations.

Package path: `./packages/pg`

Exports:
- `.` -> `./mod.ts`
- `./ddl` -> `./migrate/ddl.ts`
- `./migrate` -> `./migrate/mod.ts`
- `./orm` -> `./orm/mod.ts`

### @sisal/sqlite 0.4.0

SQLite adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations.

Package path: `./packages/sqlite`

Exports:
- `.` -> `./mod.ts`
- `./ddl` -> `./migrate/ddl.ts`
- `./migrate` -> `./migrate/mod.ts`
- `./orm` -> `./orm/mod.ts`

### @sisal/libsql 0.4.0

libSQL and Turso adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations.

Package path: `./packages/libsql`

Exports:
- `.` -> `./mod.ts`
- `./ddl` -> `./migrate/ddl.ts`
- `./migrate` -> `./migrate/mod.ts`
- `./orm` -> `./orm/mod.ts`

### @sisal/neon 0.4.0

Neon serverless PostgreSQL adapter boundaries for Sisal ORM and migrations.

Package path: `./packages/neon`

Exports:
- `.` -> `./mod.ts`
- `./ddl` -> `./ddl.ts`
- `./migrate` -> `./migrate/mod.ts`
- `./orm` -> `./orm/mod.ts`

## Public API Symbol Index

### @sisal/orm . (packages/orm/mod.ts)

Small typed SQL and ORM primitives for Sisal database tooling.

- `and` (function): Combines conditions with SQL AND, ignoring nullish values.
- `AnyTableDefinition` (typeAlias): Any Sisal table definition, regardless of its column map.
- `arrayContained` (function): Postgres array <@ — true when column is contained by value.
- `arrayContains` (function): Postgres array @> — true when column contains every element of value. SQLite/libSQL have no array containment operator (rendering throws there).
- `arrayOverlaps` (function): Postgres array && — true when column and value share any element.
- `asc` (function): Ascending order term for orderBy, e.g. orderBy(asc(users.columns.name)).
- `assertValidSchemaSnapshot` (function): Throws when a schema snapshot is invalid.
- `AtomicOperation` (interface): A portable atomic operation — a "transaction script". Author dependent read-modify-write steps once; {@link AtomicOperation.run} executes them as a single transaction on any adapter (@sisal/pg, @sisal/neon,...
- `avg` (function): avg(column) aggregate expression.
- `BatchStatement` (typeAlias): Statement accepted by Database.batch: a query builder, Sql fragment, or already-rendered SqlQuery.
- `between` (function): column BETWEEN min AND max SQL condition (inclusive).
- `check` (function): A named CHECK constraint from a sql expression.
- `ColumnArray` (typeAlias): Array element/column type produced by {@link ColumnBuilder.array}.
- `ColumnBuilder` (interface): Immutable column builder used to define table schemas.
- `ColumnDataType` (typeAlias): Column data types supported by the built-in column builder factory.
- `ColumnDefinition` (interface): Column metadata used by table definitions and future adapters.
- `ColumnDefinitionFromBuilder` (typeAlias): Materialized column metadata inferred from a {@link ColumnBuilder}.
- `ColumnName` (typeAlias): Normalized simple column name.
- `ColumnNamingStrategy` (typeAlias): A column-naming strategy: how a JavaScript property key maps to the physical SQL column name when a column does not declare an explicit .named(...).
- `ColumnRuntimeType` (typeAlias): JavaScript runtime value types represented by ORM columns.
- `columns` (variable): Column builder factory for table schemas.
- `ColumnValueMode` (typeAlias): Runtime value mode for date/time-ish columns.
- `CompoundSelectBuilder` (interface): A combined query produced by a set operation. Trailing orderBy/limit/ offset apply to the whole compound, and further set operations may be chained.
- `Condition` (interface): Boolean SQL condition wrapper used by query builders.
- `count` (function): count(*) (no argument) or count(column) aggregate expression.
- `countDistinct` (function): count(distinct column) aggregate expression.
- `createColumn` (function): Creates a named column definition from metadata.
- `createDatabase` (function): Creates a database facade from a driver and dialect.
- `createSchemaSnapshot` (function): Creates a stable Sisal schema snapshot from ORM table metadata.
- `CreateSchemaSnapshotInput` (interface): Input accepted by {@link createSchemaSnapshot}.
- `CreateSchemaSnapshotOptions` (interface): Options applied when building a schema snapshot from ORM tables.
- `Cte` (typeAlias): A named common table expression. Its keys are the projected columns of the inner query, each usable as a column reference in select, where, etc. Create one with {@link Database.$with} and reference it via {@link...
- `CteBuilder` (interface): Intermediate returned by {@link Database.$with}; complete it with .as.
- `CustomColumnTypeOptions` (interface): Trusted custom column type metadata for {@link columns.customType}.
- `Database` (interface): Database facade used by query builders and manual SQL execution.
- `DatabaseOptions` (interface): Options for creating a {@link Database}.
- `DatabaseQuery` (typeAlias): Callable raw-query function plus schema keyed relational query helpers.
- `DatabaseSchema` (typeAlias): Schema map used to expose db.query. relational helpers.
- `DateColumnMode` (typeAlias): Runtime modes for SQL date columns.
- `defineAtomicOperation` (function): Defines an {@link AtomicOperation} from a transaction-scoped body. The body receives a transaction-scoped {@link Database} plus the typed input and may run dependent steps (a read that drives a later write). The same...
- `defineFunction` (function): Declares a typed database function: its positional argument column types and its return shape (a RETURNS TABLE (...) row map or a single scalar column).
- `defineSchemaSnapshot` (function): Normalizes and validates a schema snapshot.
- `defineTable` (function): Defines a typed table schema.
- `DefineTableOptions` (interface): Options accepted by {@link defineTable}.
- `DeleteBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for DELETE queries.
- `desc` (function): Descending order term for orderBy, e.g. orderBy(desc(users.columns.id)).
- `deserializeSchemaSnapshot` (function): Parses canonical JSON produced by {@link serializeSchemaSnapshot}, then normalizes and validates it. Throws when the JSON is malformed or the resulting snapshot is invalid, so a successful call always returns a...
- `diffSchemaSnapshots` (function): Computes the structural difference from one snapshot to another.
- `emptySql` (function): Returns an empty SQL fragment.
- `eq` (function): column = value SQL condition.
- `equalSchemaSnapshots` (function): Returns true when two snapshots are structurally equal after normalization.
- `exists` (function): EXISTS (subquery) — true when the subquery returns any row.
- `ForLockOptions` (interface): Options for {@link SelectBuilder.for} row-level locking.
- `FunctionArgsConfig` (typeAlias): Column-builder map describing a function's positional arguments.
- `FunctionArgsInput` (typeAlias): The positional-argument input object inferred from a function's args.
- `FunctionCall` (interface): The result of {@link Database.call}: a renderable, runnable function call.
- `FunctionConfig` (interface): Config object accepted by {@link defineFunction}.
- `FunctionDefinition` (interface): A typed database-function descriptor produced by {@link defineFunction}. Pass it to {@link Database.call} to render and run a single SELECT * FROM fn(args) statement. The two type parameters carry the inferred...
- `FunctionReturnsConfig` (typeAlias): A function's return shape: a column map (RETURNS TABLE (...), yielding a typed row) or a single column builder (a scalar, yielding a typed value).
- `FunctionRow` (typeAlias): The row (table return) or scalar value a function call yields.
- `getDefaultColumnNaming` (function): Returns the global default column-naming strategy applied by {@link defineTable} when a table does not pass its own naming option. Defaults to "snake_case".
- `getTableColumns` (function): Returns table columns.
- `getTableName` (function): Returns the normalized table name.
- `gt` (function): column > value SQL condition.
- `gte` (function): column >= value SQL condition.
- `identifier` (function): Creates a validated SQL identifier fragment.
- `ilike` (function): Case-insensitive ILIKE match. Renders ILIKE on Postgres and degrades to the (case-insensitive) LIKE on SQLite/libSQL/MySQL, which have no ILIKE.
- `inArray` (function): column IN (...). Each value is a bound parameter. An empty array yields a constant always-false condition (1 = 0) rather than invalid IN () SQL, so dynamic filters with no values are safe. A select builder renders as...
- `index` (function): A table index (CREATE INDEX); complete it with .on(...columns).
- `IndexColumnSpec` (typeAlias): A single key in a table index produced by index().on(...): either a column (optionally with a sort direction) or a raw SQL expression.
- `IndexConstraintBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder returned by {@link index} / {@link uniqueIndex}.
- `InferInsert` (typeAlias): Infers the accepted insert shape for a table, honoring defaults and optional fields.
- `InferProjection` (typeAlias): Inferred row type for a projected select.
- `InferSelect` (typeAlias): Infers the row shape returned when selecting from a table.
- `InsertBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for INSERT queries.
- `InsertValues` (typeAlias): Insert row values: each column accepts its literal type or a {@link Sql} expression. Literals bind as parameters; Sql values render inline.
- `isColumn` (function): Returns true when a value has the public column definition shape.
- `isEmptySchemaSnapshotDiff` (function): Returns true when a snapshot diff contains no table or column changes.
- `isNotNull` (function): column IS NOT NULL SQL condition.
- `isNull` (function): column IS NULL SQL condition.
- `isOrderTerm` (function): Returns true when a value is an asc()/desc() {@link OrderTerm}.
- `isSql` (function): Returns true when a value is a Sisal SQL fragment.
- `isSqlQuery` (function): Returns true when a value is driver-ready SQL text and parameters.
- `isTable` (function): Returns true when a value has the public table definition shape.
- `joinSql` (function): Joins SQL fragments with a separator.
- `KeysetCursor` (typeAlias): The cursor shape for a keyset over TResult, keyed by the ordered columns.
- `KeysetKeys` (typeAlias): The union of JS property keys carried by a tuple of {@link OrderTerm}s.
- `KeysetOptions` (interface): Options for {@link SelectBuilder.keyset}.
- `KeysetPage` (interface): A page returned by a keyset query: the rows plus the cursor for the next page.
- `KeysetSelectBuilder` (interface): A keyset-paginated select; set the page size with .limit(n) then run it.
- `like` (function): column LIKE value SQL condition.
- `Logger` (interface): Minimal logger contract accepted by Sisal packages.
- `LoggerMethod` (interface): Minimal logger contracts accepted by Sisal packages.
- `lt` (function): column < value SQL condition.
- `lte` (function): column <= value SQL condition.
- `max` (function): max(column) aggregate expression.
- `memoryOrmDriver` (function): Creates a tiny in-memory driver that records no data and returns empty rows.
- `MemoryOrmDriverOptions` (interface): Options for the in-memory ORM driver.
- `min` (function): min(column) aggregate expression.
- `ne` (function): column <> value SQL condition.
- `noopOrmDriver` (function): Creates a driver that never touches a real database.
- `normalizeColumnName` (function): Normalizes a simple column name.
- `normalizeSchemaSnapshot` (function): Returns a deterministic clone of a schema snapshot.
- `normalizeSqlInput` (function): Normalizes manual or builder SQL into the driver query contract.
- `normalizeTableName` (function): Normalizes a table name.
- `normalizeTemporalSqlValue` (function): Normalizes Temporal values nested inside arrays before driver execution.
- `not` (function): Negates a SQL condition with NOT (...).
- `notBetween` (function): column NOT BETWEEN min AND max SQL condition.
- `notExists` (function): NOT EXISTS (subquery) — true when the subquery returns no rows.
- `notIlike` (function): Case-insensitive NOT ILIKE; degrades to NOT LIKE off Postgres.
- `notInArray` (function): column NOT IN (...). An empty array yields a constant always-true condition; a select builder renders as a subquery.
- `notLike` (function): column NOT LIKE value SQL condition.
- `or` (function): Combines conditions with SQL OR, ignoring nullish values.
- `OrderTerm` (interface): An ORDER BY term produced by asc()/desc(). It is a SQL fragment ("t"."col" asc) that also carries the underlying column and its direction, so keyset pagination can build the matching comparison predicate and derive a...
- `OrmDriver` (interface): Async-first driver contract for future database adapters.
- `OrmError` (class): Error thrown for schema, SQL, execution, and transaction failures.
- `OrmErrorCode` (typeAlias): Error codes emitted by ORM schema, SQL, driver, and transaction helpers.
- `OrmErrorOptions` (interface): Options accepted when constructing an {@link OrmError}.
- `OrmQueryResult` (interface): Result returned by ORM drivers and database execution methods.
- `OrmTransaction` (interface): Driver transaction facade exposed to transaction callbacks.
- `placeholder` (function): Creates a named placeholder fragment for a prepared statement.
- `PlaceholderValues` (typeAlias): Placeholder name → bound value map supplied when running a prepared query.
- `PreparedQuery` (interface): A query rendered once that is executed many times with different {@link placeholder} values bound in.
- `primaryKey` (function): A composite/table-level primary key over the given columns.
- `quoteIdentifier` (function): Quotes and validates a SQL identifier for a dialect.
- `raw` (function): Creates an unsafe raw SQL fragment.
- `RawQueryExecutor` (interface): A raw SQL query executor.
- `redactErrorCause` (function): Returns a cause with any secrets in its message/stack masked. The original is kept when there is nothing to redact, so ordinary debugging is unaffected; only credential-bearing errors are replaced with a sanitized...
- `redactSecrets` (function): Masks credentials in a string: passwords in a URL's userinfo and the values of credential-bearing parameters (password, authToken, token, apiKey, secret, …). Use it before logging or surfacing anything that may...
- `ReferentialAction` (typeAlias): A foreign-key referential action for ON DELETE / ON UPDATE.
- `ReferentialOptions` (interface): Options accepted by {@link ColumnBuilder.references}.
- `RelationalColumnSelection` (typeAlias): Column selection accepted by relational queries.
- `RelationalFindOptions` (interface): Options accepted by db.query..findMany/findFirst.
- `RelationalQueryResult` (typeAlias): Result type for relational queries after columns and with are applied.
- `RelationalTableQuery` (interface): Query helpers exposed at db.query..
- `RelationConfig` (interface): Explicit relation column mapping.
- `RelationDefinition` (interface): Relation metadata produced by {@link relations}.
- `RelationDefinitionMap` (typeAlias): Relation map returned from a relations(table, ...) callback.
- `RelationHelpers` (interface): Helpers passed to {@link relations}.
- `RelationMode` (typeAlias): One-to-one or one-to-many relation shape.
- `relations` (function): Defines named relations for a table, Drizzle-style.
- `RelationsList` (typeAlias): Relational metadata list accepted by {@link createDatabase}.
- `renderSql` (function): Renders a SQL fragment into driver text and parameter array.
- `SCHEMA_SNAPSHOT_VERSION` (variable): Public entrypoint for @sisal/orm.
- `SelectBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for SELECT queries.
- `SelectColumnRef` (interface): A column reference usable in a select projection.
- `SelectProjection` (typeAlias): Map of result key to selected column or expression, for db.select({ ... }).
- `SelectProjectionValue` (typeAlias): A value usable in a select projection: a column reference, a SQL expression, or a select/compound builder embedded as a scalar subquery.
- `serializeSchemaSnapshot` (function): Serializes a snapshot to canonical JSON.
- `serializeSqlValue` (function): Converts a JS value into a SQL parameter.
- `setDefaultColumnNaming` (function): Sets the global default column-naming strategy for **future** {@link defineTable} calls. Tables already defined keep the name they were built with, so call this before defining your tables (e.g. at the top of your...
- `SetOperand` (typeAlias): A query usable as the right-hand operand of a set operation (union, intersect, except). Both {@link SelectBuilder} and {@link CompoundSelectBuilder} satisfy this shape.
- `SisalCheckConstraintSnapshot` (interface): Serializable check-constraint descriptor.
- `SisalColumnDefault` (typeAlias): Serializable column default value or expression.
- `SisalColumnDiff` (interface): A column present in both tables whose definition changed.
- `SisalColumnSnapshot` (interface): Serializable column definition inside a table snapshot.
- `SisalColumnType` (interface): Dialect-neutral column type descriptor.
- `SisalDialectName` (typeAlias): SQL dialect names understood by Sisal schema snapshots.
- `SisalError` (class): Small structured error primitive shared by Sisal packages.
- `SisalErrorOptions` (interface): Options accepted by {@link SisalError}.
- `SisalErrorSeverity` (typeAlias): Severity attached to structured Sisal errors.
- `SisalForeignKeySnapshot` (interface): Serializable foreign-key constraint descriptor.
- `SisalIndexColumnSnapshot` (interface): A single key in an index: a column or a raw SQL expression, with an optional sort direction.
- `SisalIndexSnapshot` (interface): Serializable index descriptor.
- `SisalPrimaryKeySnapshot` (interface): Serializable primary-key constraint descriptor.
- `SisalSchemaIssue` (interface): One validation issue found in a schema snapshot.
- `SisalSchemaIssueCode` (typeAlias): Validation issue codes emitted by schema snapshot helpers.
- `SisalSchemaSnapshot` (interface): Serializable database schema snapshot shared by Sisal data packages.
- `SisalSchemaSnapshotDiff` (interface): Structural difference between two schema snapshots (from → to).
- `SisalTableColumnsDiff` (interface): Column-level changes between two versions of one table.
- `SisalTableDiff` (interface): A table present in both snapshots whose definition changed.
- `SisalTableSnapshot` (interface): Serializable table definition inside a schema snapshot.
- `SisalUniqueConstraintSnapshot` (interface): Serializable unique-constraint descriptor.
- `sql` (function): SQL tagged template that keeps interpolated values as parameters.
- `Sql` (interface): SQL fragment made from safe chunks and separate parameters.
- `SqlChunk` (typeAlias): Internal chunk representation used by parameterized SQL fragments.
- `SqlDialect` (typeAlias): SQL dialect names supported by SQL rendering helpers.
- `SqlExpression` (interface): A typed SQL expression (e.g. an aggregate like {@link count}) usable as a value in a select projection. The phantom type parameter drives the inferred result type for that projected key.
- `SqlInput` (typeAlias): Any SQL input accepted by database execution methods.
- `SqlParameter` (typeAlias): Parameter value shape accepted by rendered SQL queries.
- `SqlQuery` (interface): Driver-ready SQL text and parameter array.
- `Subquery` (typeAlias): A query aliased as a derived table via {@link SelectBuilder.as}. Its keys are the inner query's projected columns, each usable as a reference once the subquery is passed to .from(...).
- `SubquerySource` (interface): A select/compound builder usable as a subquery (derived table or scalar).
- `sum` (function): sum(column) aggregate expression.
- `TableColumn` (typeAlias): Materialized column belonging to a table definition.
- `TableColumns` (typeAlias): Column builder map passed to {@link defineTable}.
- `TableConstraint` (typeAlias): A table-level constraint or index produced by the {@link defineTable} extras callback — index/uniqueIndex, primaryKey, unique, or check.
- `TableDefinition` (interface): Table schema definition returned by defineTable.
- `TableName` (typeAlias): Normalized table name, optionally including a validated schema path.
- `TableRelations` (interface): Relation collection for one table.
- `TemporalParsingOptions` (interface): Options for Temporal result parsing in a database facade.
- `TemporalSqlValue` (typeAlias): Temporal values accepted as SQL parameters.
- `TimeColumnMode` (typeAlias): Runtime modes for SQL time columns.
- `TimestampColumnMode` (typeAlias): Runtime modes for SQL timestamp / timestamptz columns.
- `toSql` (function): Converts a SQL fragment or condition into SQL.
- `unique` (function): A named/composite UNIQUE constraint; complete it with .on(...columns).
- `UniqueConstraintBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder returned by {@link unique}.
- `uniqueIndex` (function): A unique table index (CREATE UNIQUE INDEX); complete with .on(...).
- `UpdateBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for UPDATE queries.
- `UpdateValues` (typeAlias): Update/upsert assignments: each column set to its literal type or a Sql expression, all columns optional (UPDATE ... SET).
- `validateSchemaSnapshot` (function): Returns validation issues for a schema snapshot without mutating it.
- `WithQueryBuilder` (interface): Query root seeded with CTEs, returned by {@link Database.with}.

### @sisal/orm ./core (packages/orm/core/mod.ts)

Typed SQL builders and ORM driver contracts for @sisal/orm.

- `and` (function): Combines conditions with SQL AND, ignoring nullish values.
- `AnyTableDefinition` (typeAlias): Any Sisal table definition, regardless of its column map.
- `arrayContained` (function): Postgres array <@ — true when column is contained by value.
- `arrayContains` (function): Postgres array @> — true when column contains every element of value. SQLite/libSQL have no array containment operator (rendering throws there).
- `arrayOverlaps` (function): Postgres array && — true when column and value share any element.
- `asc` (function): Ascending order term for orderBy, e.g. orderBy(asc(users.columns.name)).
- `AtomicOperation` (interface): A portable atomic operation — a "transaction script". Author dependent read-modify-write steps once; {@link AtomicOperation.run} executes them as a single transaction on any adapter (@sisal/pg, @sisal/neon,...
- `avg` (function): avg(column) aggregate expression.
- `BatchStatement` (typeAlias): Statement accepted by Database.batch: a query builder, Sql fragment, or already-rendered SqlQuery.
- `between` (function): column BETWEEN min AND max SQL condition (inclusive).
- `check` (function): A named CHECK constraint from a sql expression.
- `ColumnArray` (typeAlias): Array element/column type produced by {@link ColumnBuilder.array}.
- `ColumnBuilder` (interface): Immutable column builder used to define table schemas.
- `ColumnDataType` (typeAlias): Column data types supported by the built-in column builder factory.
- `ColumnDefinition` (interface): Column metadata used by table definitions and future adapters.
- `ColumnDefinitionFromBuilder` (typeAlias): Materialized column metadata inferred from a {@link ColumnBuilder}.
- `ColumnName` (typeAlias): Normalized simple column name.
- `ColumnNamingStrategy` (typeAlias): A column-naming strategy: how a JavaScript property key maps to the physical SQL column name when a column does not declare an explicit .named(...).
- `ColumnRuntimeType` (typeAlias): JavaScript runtime value types represented by ORM columns.
- `columns` (variable): Column builder factory for table schemas.
- `ColumnValueMode` (typeAlias): Runtime value mode for date/time-ish columns.
- `CompoundSelectBuilder` (interface): A combined query produced by a set operation. Trailing orderBy/limit/ offset apply to the whole compound, and further set operations may be chained.
- `Condition` (interface): Boolean SQL condition wrapper used by query builders.
- `count` (function): count(*) (no argument) or count(column) aggregate expression.
- `countDistinct` (function): count(distinct column) aggregate expression.
- `createColumn` (function): Creates a named column definition from metadata.
- `createDatabase` (function): Creates a database facade from a driver and dialect.
- `createSchemaSnapshot` (function): Creates a stable Sisal schema snapshot from ORM table metadata.
- `CreateSchemaSnapshotInput` (interface): Input accepted by {@link createSchemaSnapshot}.
- `CreateSchemaSnapshotOptions` (interface): Options applied when building a schema snapshot from ORM tables.
- `Cte` (typeAlias): A named common table expression. Its keys are the projected columns of the inner query, each usable as a column reference in select, where, etc. Create one with {@link Database.$with} and reference it via {@link...
- `CteBuilder` (interface): Intermediate returned by {@link Database.$with}; complete it with .as.
- `CustomColumnTypeOptions` (interface): Trusted custom column type metadata for {@link columns.customType}.
- `Database` (interface): Database facade used by query builders and manual SQL execution.
- `DatabaseOptions` (interface): Options for creating a {@link Database}.
- `DatabaseQuery` (typeAlias): Callable raw-query function plus schema keyed relational query helpers.
- `DatabaseSchema` (typeAlias): Schema map used to expose db.query. relational helpers.
- `DateColumnMode` (typeAlias): Runtime modes for SQL date columns.
- `defineAtomicOperation` (function): Defines an {@link AtomicOperation} from a transaction-scoped body. The body receives a transaction-scoped {@link Database} plus the typed input and may run dependent steps (a read that drives a later write). The same...
- `defineFunction` (function): Declares a typed database function: its positional argument column types and its return shape (a RETURNS TABLE (...) row map or a single scalar column).
- `defineTable` (function): Defines a typed table schema.
- `DefineTableOptions` (interface): Options accepted by {@link defineTable}.
- `DeleteBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for DELETE queries.
- `desc` (function): Descending order term for orderBy, e.g. orderBy(desc(users.columns.id)).
- `emptySql` (function): Returns an empty SQL fragment.
- `eq` (function): column = value SQL condition.
- `exists` (function): EXISTS (subquery) — true when the subquery returns any row.
- `ForLockOptions` (interface): Options for {@link SelectBuilder.for} row-level locking.
- `FunctionArgsConfig` (typeAlias): Column-builder map describing a function's positional arguments.
- `FunctionArgsInput` (typeAlias): The positional-argument input object inferred from a function's args.
- `FunctionCall` (interface): The result of {@link Database.call}: a renderable, runnable function call.
- `FunctionConfig` (interface): Config object accepted by {@link defineFunction}.
- `FunctionDefinition` (interface): A typed database-function descriptor produced by {@link defineFunction}. Pass it to {@link Database.call} to render and run a single SELECT * FROM fn(args) statement. The two type parameters carry the inferred...
- `FunctionReturnsConfig` (typeAlias): A function's return shape: a column map (RETURNS TABLE (...), yielding a typed row) or a single column builder (a scalar, yielding a typed value).
- `FunctionRow` (typeAlias): The row (table return) or scalar value a function call yields.
- `getDefaultColumnNaming` (function): Returns the global default column-naming strategy applied by {@link defineTable} when a table does not pass its own naming option. Defaults to "snake_case".
- `getTableColumns` (function): Returns table columns.
- `getTableName` (function): Returns the normalized table name.
- `gt` (function): column > value SQL condition.
- `gte` (function): column >= value SQL condition.
- `identifier` (function): Creates a validated SQL identifier fragment.
- `ilike` (function): Case-insensitive ILIKE match. Renders ILIKE on Postgres and degrades to the (case-insensitive) LIKE on SQLite/libSQL/MySQL, which have no ILIKE.
- `inArray` (function): column IN (...). Each value is a bound parameter. An empty array yields a constant always-false condition (1 = 0) rather than invalid IN () SQL, so dynamic filters with no values are safe. A select builder renders as...
- `index` (function): A table index (CREATE INDEX); complete it with .on(...columns).
- `IndexColumnSpec` (typeAlias): A single key in a table index produced by index().on(...): either a column (optionally with a sort direction) or a raw SQL expression.
- `IndexConstraintBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder returned by {@link index} / {@link uniqueIndex}.
- `InferInsert` (typeAlias): Infers the accepted insert shape for a table, honoring defaults and optional fields.
- `InferProjection` (typeAlias): Inferred row type for a projected select.
- `InferSelect` (typeAlias): Infers the row shape returned when selecting from a table.
- `InsertBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for INSERT queries.
- `InsertValues` (typeAlias): Insert row values: each column accepts its literal type or a {@link Sql} expression. Literals bind as parameters; Sql values render inline.
- `isColumn` (function): Returns true when a value has the public column definition shape.
- `isNotNull` (function): column IS NOT NULL SQL condition.
- `isNull` (function): column IS NULL SQL condition.
- `isOrderTerm` (function): Returns true when a value is an asc()/desc() {@link OrderTerm}.
- `isSql` (function): Returns true when a value is a Sisal SQL fragment.
- `isSqlQuery` (function): Returns true when a value is driver-ready SQL text and parameters.
- `isTable` (function): Returns true when a value has the public table definition shape.
- `joinSql` (function): Joins SQL fragments with a separator.
- `KeysetCursor` (typeAlias): The cursor shape for a keyset over TResult, keyed by the ordered columns.
- `KeysetKeys` (typeAlias): The union of JS property keys carried by a tuple of {@link OrderTerm}s.
- `KeysetOptions` (interface): Options for {@link SelectBuilder.keyset}.
- `KeysetPage` (interface): A page returned by a keyset query: the rows plus the cursor for the next page.
- `KeysetSelectBuilder` (interface): A keyset-paginated select; set the page size with .limit(n) then run it.
- `like` (function): column LIKE value SQL condition.
- `lt` (function): column < value SQL condition.
- `lte` (function): column <= value SQL condition.
- `max` (function): max(column) aggregate expression.
- `memoryOrmDriver` (function): Creates a tiny in-memory driver that records no data and returns empty rows.
- `MemoryOrmDriverOptions` (interface): Options for the in-memory ORM driver.
- `min` (function): min(column) aggregate expression.
- `ne` (function): column <> value SQL condition.
- `noopOrmDriver` (function): Creates a driver that never touches a real database.
- `normalizeColumnName` (function): Normalizes a simple column name.
- `normalizeSqlInput` (function): Normalizes manual or builder SQL into the driver query contract.
- `normalizeTableName` (function): Normalizes a table name.
- `normalizeTemporalSqlValue` (function): Normalizes Temporal values nested inside arrays before driver execution.
- `not` (function): Negates a SQL condition with NOT (...).
- `notBetween` (function): column NOT BETWEEN min AND max SQL condition.
- `notExists` (function): NOT EXISTS (subquery) — true when the subquery returns no rows.
- `notIlike` (function): Case-insensitive NOT ILIKE; degrades to NOT LIKE off Postgres.
- `notInArray` (function): column NOT IN (...). An empty array yields a constant always-true condition; a select builder renders as a subquery.
- `notLike` (function): column NOT LIKE value SQL condition.
- `or` (function): Combines conditions with SQL OR, ignoring nullish values.
- `OrderTerm` (interface): An ORDER BY term produced by asc()/desc(). It is a SQL fragment ("t"."col" asc) that also carries the underlying column and its direction, so keyset pagination can build the matching comparison predicate and derive a...
- `OrmDriver` (interface): Async-first driver contract for future database adapters.
- `OrmError` (class): Error thrown for schema, SQL, execution, and transaction failures.
- `OrmErrorCode` (typeAlias): Error codes emitted by ORM schema, SQL, driver, and transaction helpers.
- `OrmErrorOptions` (interface): Options accepted when constructing an {@link OrmError}.
- `OrmQueryResult` (interface): Result returned by ORM drivers and database execution methods.
- `OrmTransaction` (interface): Driver transaction facade exposed to transaction callbacks.
- `placeholder` (function): Creates a named placeholder fragment for a prepared statement.
- `PlaceholderValues` (typeAlias): Placeholder name → bound value map supplied when running a prepared query.
- `PreparedQuery` (interface): A query rendered once that is executed many times with different {@link placeholder} values bound in.
- `primaryKey` (function): A composite/table-level primary key over the given columns.
- `quoteIdentifier` (function): Quotes and validates a SQL identifier for a dialect.
- `raw` (function): Creates an unsafe raw SQL fragment.
- `RawQueryExecutor` (interface): A raw SQL query executor.
- `ReferentialAction` (typeAlias): A foreign-key referential action for ON DELETE / ON UPDATE.
- `ReferentialOptions` (interface): Options accepted by {@link ColumnBuilder.references}.
- `RelationalColumnSelection` (typeAlias): Column selection accepted by relational queries.
- `RelationalFindOptions` (interface): Options accepted by db.query..findMany/findFirst.
- `RelationalQueryResult` (typeAlias): Result type for relational queries after columns and with are applied.
- `RelationalTableQuery` (interface): Query helpers exposed at db.query..
- `RelationConfig` (interface): Explicit relation column mapping.
- `RelationDefinition` (interface): Relation metadata produced by {@link relations}.
- `RelationDefinitionMap` (typeAlias): Relation map returned from a relations(table, ...) callback.
- `RelationHelpers` (interface): Helpers passed to {@link relations}.
- `RelationMode` (typeAlias): One-to-one or one-to-many relation shape.
- `relations` (function): Defines named relations for a table, Drizzle-style.
- `RelationsList` (typeAlias): Relational metadata list accepted by {@link createDatabase}.
- `renderSql` (function): Renders a SQL fragment into driver text and parameter array.
- `SelectBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for SELECT queries.
- `SelectColumnRef` (interface): A column reference usable in a select projection.
- `SelectProjection` (typeAlias): Map of result key to selected column or expression, for db.select({ ... }).
- `SelectProjectionValue` (typeAlias): A value usable in a select projection: a column reference, a SQL expression, or a select/compound builder embedded as a scalar subquery.
- `serializeSqlValue` (function): Converts a JS value into a SQL parameter.
- `setDefaultColumnNaming` (function): Sets the global default column-naming strategy for **future** {@link defineTable} calls. Tables already defined keep the name they were built with, so call this before defining your tables (e.g. at the top of your...
- `SetOperand` (typeAlias): A query usable as the right-hand operand of a set operation (union, intersect, except). Both {@link SelectBuilder} and {@link CompoundSelectBuilder} satisfy this shape.
- `sql` (function): SQL tagged template that keeps interpolated values as parameters.
- `Sql` (interface): SQL fragment made from safe chunks and separate parameters.
- `SqlChunk` (typeAlias): Internal chunk representation used by parameterized SQL fragments.
- `SqlDialect` (typeAlias): SQL dialect names supported by SQL rendering helpers.
- `SqlExpression` (interface): A typed SQL expression (e.g. an aggregate like {@link count}) usable as a value in a select projection. The phantom type parameter drives the inferred result type for that projected key.
- `SqlInput` (typeAlias): Any SQL input accepted by database execution methods.
- `SqlParameter` (typeAlias): Parameter value shape accepted by rendered SQL queries.
- `SqlQuery` (interface): Driver-ready SQL text and parameter array.
- `Subquery` (typeAlias): A query aliased as a derived table via {@link SelectBuilder.as}. Its keys are the inner query's projected columns, each usable as a reference once the subquery is passed to .from(...).
- `SubquerySource` (interface): A select/compound builder usable as a subquery (derived table or scalar).
- `sum` (function): sum(column) aggregate expression.
- `TableColumn` (typeAlias): Materialized column belonging to a table definition.
- `TableColumns` (typeAlias): Column builder map passed to {@link defineTable}.
- `TableConstraint` (typeAlias): A table-level constraint or index produced by the {@link defineTable} extras callback — index/uniqueIndex, primaryKey, unique, or check.
- `TableDefinition` (interface): Table schema definition returned by defineTable.
- `TableName` (typeAlias): Normalized table name, optionally including a validated schema path.
- `TableRelations` (interface): Relation collection for one table.
- `TemporalParsingOptions` (interface): Options for Temporal result parsing in a database facade.
- `TemporalSqlValue` (typeAlias): Temporal values accepted as SQL parameters.
- `TimeColumnMode` (typeAlias): Runtime modes for SQL time columns.
- `TimestampColumnMode` (typeAlias): Runtime modes for SQL timestamp / timestamptz columns.
- `toSql` (function): Converts a SQL fragment or condition into SQL.
- `unique` (function): A named/composite UNIQUE constraint; complete it with .on(...columns).
- `UniqueConstraintBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder returned by {@link unique}.
- `uniqueIndex` (function): A unique table index (CREATE UNIQUE INDEX); complete with .on(...).
- `UpdateBuilder` (interface): Fluent builder for UPDATE queries.
- `UpdateValues` (typeAlias): Update/upsert assignments: each column set to its literal type or a Sql expression, all columns optional (UPDATE ... SET).
- `WithQueryBuilder` (interface): Query root seeded with CTEs, returned by {@link Database.with}.

### @sisal/orm ./error (packages/orm/error.ts)

Structured error primitives shared by Sisal packages.

- `redactErrorCause` (function): Returns a cause with any secrets in its message/stack masked. The original is kept when there is nothing to redact, so ordinary debugging is unaffected; only credential-bearing errors are replaced with a sanitized...
- `redactSecrets` (function): Masks credentials in a string: passwords in a URL's userinfo and the values of credential-bearing parameters (password, authToken, token, apiKey, secret, …). Use it before logging or surfacing anything that may...
- `SisalError` (class): Small structured error primitive shared by Sisal packages.
- `SisalErrorOptions` (interface): Options accepted by {@link SisalError}.
- `SisalErrorSeverity` (typeAlias): Severity attached to structured Sisal errors.

### @sisal/orm ./logger (packages/orm/logger.ts)

Minimal logger contracts accepted by Sisal packages.

- `Logger` (interface): Minimal logger contract accepted by Sisal packages.
- `LoggerMethod` (interface): Minimal logger contracts accepted by Sisal packages.

### @sisal/orm ./schema (packages/orm/schema.ts)

Public entrypoint for @sisal/orm.

- `assertValidSchemaSnapshot` (function): Throws when a schema snapshot is invalid.
- `defineSchemaSnapshot` (function): Normalizes and validates a schema snapshot.
- `deserializeSchemaSnapshot` (function): Parses canonical JSON produced by {@link serializeSchemaSnapshot}, then normalizes and validates it. Throws when the JSON is malformed or the resulting snapshot is invalid, so a successful call always returns a...
- `diffSchemaSnapshots` (function): Computes the structural difference from one snapshot to another.
- `equalSchemaSnapshots` (function): Returns true when two snapshots are structurally equal after normalization.
- `isEmptySchemaSnapshotDiff` (function): Returns true when a snapshot diff contains no table or column changes.
- `normalizeSchemaSnapshot` (function): Returns a deterministic clone of a schema snapshot.
- `SCHEMA_SNAPSHOT_VERSION` (variable): Public entrypoint for @sisal/orm.
- `serializeSchemaSnapshot` (function): Serializes a snapshot to canonical JSON.
- `SisalCheckConstraintSnapshot` (interface): Serializable check-constraint descriptor.
- `SisalColumnDefault` (typeAlias): Serializable column default value or expression.
- `SisalColumnDiff` (interface): A column present in both tables whose definition changed.
- `SisalColumnSnapshot` (interface): Serializable column definition inside a table snapshot.
- `SisalColumnType` (interface): Dialect-neutral column type descriptor.
- `SisalDialectName` (typeAlias): SQL dialect names understood by Sisal schema snapshots.
- `SisalForeignKeySnapshot` (interface): Serializable foreign-key constraint descriptor.
- `SisalIndexColumnSnapshot` (interface): A single key in an index: a column or a raw SQL expression, with an optional sort direction.
- `SisalIndexSnapshot` (interface): Serializable index descriptor.
- `SisalPrimaryKeySnapshot` (interface): Serializable primary-key constraint descriptor.
- `SisalSchemaIssue` (interface): One validation issue found in a schema snapshot.
- `SisalSchemaIssueCode` (typeAlias): Validation issue codes emitted by schema snapshot helpers.
- `SisalSchemaSnapshot` (interface): Serializable database schema snapshot shared by Sisal data packages.
- `SisalSchemaSnapshotDiff` (interface): Structural difference between two schema snapshots (from → to).
- `SisalTableColumnsDiff` (interface): Column-level changes between two versions of one table.
- `SisalTableDiff` (interface): A table present in both snapshots whose definition changed.
- `SisalTableSnapshot` (interface): Serializable table definition inside a schema snapshot.
- `SisalUniqueConstraintSnapshot` (interface): Serializable unique-constraint descriptor.
- `validateSchemaSnapshot` (function): Returns validation issues for a schema snapshot without mutating it.

### @sisal/migrate . (packages/migrate/mod.ts)

Migration planning and execution primitives for Sisal database tooling.

- `AppliedMigration` (interface): Applied migration history record.
- `assertMigrationChecksum` (function): Throws when an applied migration checksum differs from the current one.
- `buildMigrationFile` (function): Builds the filenames and contents for a generated migration (pure).
- `calculateMigrationChecksum` (function): Calculates a deterministic non-cryptographic checksum for a migration.
- `checkDrift` (function): Decides whether migration state is consistent (pure). Reports when the live schema has drifted from the newest captured snapshot (a migration needs to be generated), when migrations are pending, and when a .sql file...
- `createAppliedMigration` (function): Creates an applied migration history record.
- `createMigrationPlan` (function): Creates a plan from known migrations and applied history.
- `createMigrator` (function): Creates a migrator with memory store and noop driver defaults.
- `defineConfig` (function): Validates and normalizes a migration config (pure).
- `defineMigration` (function): Defines a programmatic migration and validates its public shape.
- `defineSchemaMigrationPlan` (function): Accepts validated schema snapshots as metadata for future diffing.
- `defineSqlMigration` (function): Defines a SQL migration and validates its public shape.
- `denoMigrationFileSystem` (function): Default {@link MigrationFileSystem} backed by Deno file APIs.
- `DiscoveredMigration` (interface): A migration discovered on disk by {@link readMigrationsDir}.
- `DriftCheckInput` (interface): Inputs to {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftFinding` (interface): A single drift finding from {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftKind` (typeAlias): Drift categories reported by {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftReport` (interface): Result of a drift check.
- `formatMigrationFilename` (function): Builds a zero-padded, slugged migration filename such as 0002_create_users.sql. The sequence is padded to at least 4 digits.
- `GeneratedMigrationFile` (interface): A generated migration ready to be written to disk.
- `getAppliedMigrations` (function): Returns applied history for migrations known to the current codebase.
- `getPendingMigrations` (function): Returns migrations that are not present in applied history.
- `getRollbackMigrations` (function): Returns known applied migrations in rollback order.
- `isMigrationApplied` (function): Returns true if a migration id exists in applied history.
- `memoryMigrationStore` (function): Creates an in-memory migration history store for tests and scaffolding.
- `MemoryMigrationStoreOptions` (interface): Options for the in-memory migration history store.
- `MigrateConfig` (interface): Validated migration configuration (sisal.migrate.ts).
- `MigrateProvider` (typeAlias): A migration runtime provider that refines a dialect's default adapter.
- `Migration` (typeAlias): Any migration definition accepted by a Sisal migrator.
- `MigrationBase` (interface): Common metadata shared by all migration definitions.
- `MigrationChecksum` (typeAlias): Deterministic checksum used to detect edited migrations.
- `MigrationContext` (interface): Context passed to programmatic migrations.
- `MigrationDirection` (typeAlias): Direction a migration runner is executing.
- `MigrationDownOptions` (interface): Options for rolling back applied migrations.
- `MigrationDriver` (interface): Minimal async driver contract for future database adapters.
- `MigrationError` (class): Error thrown for migration validation, planning, locking, and execution.
- `MigrationErrorCode` (typeAlias): Error codes emitted by migration validation, planning, locking, and execution.
- `MigrationErrorOptions` (interface): Options accepted when constructing a {@link MigrationError}.
- `MigrationFileSystem` (interface): Minimal filesystem surface used by the migration workflow.
- `MigrationId` (typeAlias): Stable migration identifier, usually derived from a migration file name.
- `MigrationKind` (typeAlias): Supported migration definition shapes.
- `MigrationPlan` (interface): Full migration plan grouped by pending, applied, and mismatched entries.
- `MigrationPlanItem` (interface): One migration's status inside a generated migration plan.
- `MigrationResult` (interface): Result returned after applying or rolling back migrations.
- `MigrationRunOptions` (interface): Options for applying pending migrations.
- `MigrationStatus` (typeAlias): Planning or execution status for a migration.
- `MigrationStep` (typeAlias): Executable migration step, either SQL text or a programmatic callback.
- `MigrationStore` (interface): Async-first store for applied migration history and optional locks.
- `MigrationTransaction` (interface): Driver/store scope exposed to migration transaction callbacks.
- `Migrator` (interface): Public migration runner.
- `MigratorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a core {@link Migrator}.
- `nextMigrationSequence` (function): Returns the next sequence number after the highest discovered migration.
- `noopMigrationDriver` (function): Creates a noop driver.
- `noopMigrator` (function): Creates a migrator that reports no migrations and executes nothing.
- `parseMigrationSequence` (function): Extracts the leading numeric sequence from a migration id (0002_x → 2).
- `planSchemaChanges` (function): Classifies the changes from one schema snapshot to another into ordered {@link SchemaChange}s, flagging destructive operations (drop table/column and column type changes). A missing from treats every table as newly...
- `planSchemaChangesFromDiff` (function): Classifies an already-computed {@link SisalSchemaSnapshotDiff} into ordered {@link SchemaChange}s. Use this when you already hold a diff and want to avoid recomputing it — generated DDL, for example, iterates the...
- `ProgrammaticMigration` (interface): Programmatic migration definition.
- `readMigrationsDir` (function): Reads and orders the .sql migrations in a directory (with their snapshots).
- `SchemaChange` (interface): One classified schema change, with a destructiveness flag.
- `SchemaChangeKind` (typeAlias): A single structural change between two schema snapshots.
- `SchemaMigrationChanges` (interface): Result of {@link planSchemaChanges}: an ordered, classified change list.
- `SchemaMigrationPlan` (interface): Normalized schema migration plan snapshot pair.
- `SchemaMigrationPlanInput` (interface): Input snapshots for creating a schema migration plan.
- `slugifyMigrationName` (function): Lowercases a migration name into an underscore slug (Add Users → add_users).
- `sortMigrations` (function): Returns a new migration list sorted by id.
- `splitSqlStatements` (function): Splits a SQL script into individual statements on top-level ;, ignoring semicolons inside string literals, quoted identifiers, line/block comments, and PostgreSQL dollar-quoted bodies ($$ … $$, $tag$ … $tag$). Each...
- `SqlMigration` (interface): SQL migration definition.
- `validateMigration` (function): Validates one migration definition.
- `validateMigrations` (function): Validates a migration list and rejects duplicate ids.
- `writeMigrationFile` (function): Writes a generated migration's .sql and .snapshot.json files.

### @sisal/migrate ./cli (packages/migrate/cli.ts)

sisal migration CLI.

- `CliDialect` (typeAlias): The dialects the sisal migration CLI can target.
- `CliOutput` (typeAlias): Sink for one line of CLI output — stdout by default, injectable for tests.
- `runSisalCli` (function): Runs the sisal CLI and returns a process exit code.
- `SisalCliAdapter` (interface): Dialect adapter hooks used by sisal commands.
- `SisalCliGeneratedStatements` (interface): Additive DDL generated by a dialect adapter.
- `SisalCliMigrator` (interface): Minimal migrator facade used by the CLI.
- `SisalCliOptions` (interface): Test/runtime injection points for the CLI runner.

### @sisal/migrate ./core (packages/migrate/core/mod.ts)

Migration definitions, planning, history stores, and runner contracts.

- `AppliedMigration` (interface): Applied migration history record.
- `assertMigrationChecksum` (function): Throws when an applied migration checksum differs from the current one.
- `buildMigrationFile` (function): Builds the filenames and contents for a generated migration (pure).
- `calculateMigrationChecksum` (function): Calculates a deterministic non-cryptographic checksum for a migration.
- `checkDrift` (function): Decides whether migration state is consistent (pure). Reports when the live schema has drifted from the newest captured snapshot (a migration needs to be generated), when migrations are pending, and when a .sql file...
- `createAppliedMigration` (function): Creates an applied migration history record.
- `createMigrationPlan` (function): Creates a plan from known migrations and applied history.
- `createMigrator` (function): Creates a migrator with memory store and noop driver defaults.
- `defineConfig` (function): Validates and normalizes a migration config (pure).
- `defineMigration` (function): Defines a programmatic migration and validates its public shape.
- `defineSchemaMigrationPlan` (function): Accepts validated schema snapshots as metadata for future diffing.
- `defineSqlMigration` (function): Defines a SQL migration and validates its public shape.
- `denoMigrationFileSystem` (function): Default {@link MigrationFileSystem} backed by Deno file APIs.
- `DiscoveredMigration` (interface): A migration discovered on disk by {@link readMigrationsDir}.
- `DriftCheckInput` (interface): Inputs to {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftFinding` (interface): A single drift finding from {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftKind` (typeAlias): Drift categories reported by {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftReport` (interface): Result of a drift check.
- `formatMigrationFilename` (function): Builds a zero-padded, slugged migration filename such as 0002_create_users.sql. The sequence is padded to at least 4 digits.
- `GeneratedMigrationFile` (interface): A generated migration ready to be written to disk.
- `getAppliedMigrations` (function): Returns applied history for migrations known to the current codebase.
- `getPendingMigrations` (function): Returns migrations that are not present in applied history.
- `getRollbackMigrations` (function): Returns known applied migrations in rollback order.
- `isMigrationApplied` (function): Returns true if a migration id exists in applied history.
- `memoryMigrationStore` (function): Creates an in-memory migration history store for tests and scaffolding.
- `MemoryMigrationStoreOptions` (interface): Options for the in-memory migration history store.
- `MigrateConfig` (interface): Validated migration configuration (sisal.migrate.ts).
- `MigrateProvider` (typeAlias): A migration runtime provider that refines a dialect's default adapter.
- `Migration` (typeAlias): Any migration definition accepted by a Sisal migrator.
- `MigrationBase` (interface): Common metadata shared by all migration definitions.
- `MigrationChecksum` (typeAlias): Deterministic checksum used to detect edited migrations.
- `MigrationContext` (interface): Context passed to programmatic migrations.
- `MigrationDirection` (typeAlias): Direction a migration runner is executing.
- `MigrationDownOptions` (interface): Options for rolling back applied migrations.
- `MigrationDriver` (interface): Minimal async driver contract for future database adapters.
- `MigrationError` (class): Error thrown for migration validation, planning, locking, and execution.
- `MigrationErrorCode` (typeAlias): Error codes emitted by migration validation, planning, locking, and execution.
- `MigrationErrorOptions` (interface): Options accepted when constructing a {@link MigrationError}.
- `MigrationFileSystem` (interface): Minimal filesystem surface used by the migration workflow.
- `MigrationId` (typeAlias): Stable migration identifier, usually derived from a migration file name.
- `MigrationKind` (typeAlias): Supported migration definition shapes.
- `MigrationPlan` (interface): Full migration plan grouped by pending, applied, and mismatched entries.
- `MigrationPlanItem` (interface): One migration's status inside a generated migration plan.
- `MigrationResult` (interface): Result returned after applying or rolling back migrations.
- `MigrationRunOptions` (interface): Options for applying pending migrations.
- `MigrationStatus` (typeAlias): Planning or execution status for a migration.
- `MigrationStep` (typeAlias): Executable migration step, either SQL text or a programmatic callback.
- `MigrationStore` (interface): Async-first store for applied migration history and optional locks.
- `MigrationTransaction` (interface): Driver/store scope exposed to migration transaction callbacks.
- `Migrator` (interface): Public migration runner.
- `MigratorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a core {@link Migrator}.
- `nextMigrationSequence` (function): Returns the next sequence number after the highest discovered migration.
- `noopMigrationDriver` (function): Creates a noop driver.
- `noopMigrator` (function): Creates a migrator that reports no migrations and executes nothing.
- `parseMigrationSequence` (function): Extracts the leading numeric sequence from a migration id (0002_x → 2).
- `planSchemaChanges` (function): Classifies the changes from one schema snapshot to another into ordered {@link SchemaChange}s, flagging destructive operations (drop table/column and column type changes). A missing from treats every table as newly...
- `planSchemaChangesFromDiff` (function): Classifies an already-computed {@link SisalSchemaSnapshotDiff} into ordered {@link SchemaChange}s. Use this when you already hold a diff and want to avoid recomputing it — generated DDL, for example, iterates the...
- `ProgrammaticMigration` (interface): Programmatic migration definition.
- `readMigrationsDir` (function): Reads and orders the .sql migrations in a directory (with their snapshots).
- `SchemaChange` (interface): One classified schema change, with a destructiveness flag.
- `SchemaChangeKind` (typeAlias): A single structural change between two schema snapshots.
- `SchemaMigrationChanges` (interface): Result of {@link planSchemaChanges}: an ordered, classified change list.
- `SchemaMigrationPlan` (interface): Normalized schema migration plan snapshot pair.
- `SchemaMigrationPlanInput` (interface): Input snapshots for creating a schema migration plan.
- `slugifyMigrationName` (function): Lowercases a migration name into an underscore slug (Add Users → add_users).
- `sortMigrations` (function): Returns a new migration list sorted by id.
- `splitSqlStatements` (function): Splits a SQL script into individual statements on top-level ;, ignoring semicolons inside string literals, quoted identifiers, line/block comments, and PostgreSQL dollar-quoted bodies ($$ … $$, $tag$ … $tag$). Each...
- `SqlMigration` (interface): SQL migration definition.
- `validateMigration` (function): Validates one migration definition.
- `validateMigrations` (function): Validates a migration list and rejects duplicate ids.
- `writeMigrationFile` (function): Writes a generated migration's .sql and .snapshot.json files.

### @sisal/migrate ./workflow (packages/migrate/core/workflow.ts)

SQL-first migration workflow: an injectable filesystem, generated-migration file writing/reading, config, and drift checking.

- `buildMigrationFile` (function): Builds the filenames and contents for a generated migration (pure).
- `checkDrift` (function): Decides whether migration state is consistent (pure). Reports when the live schema has drifted from the newest captured snapshot (a migration needs to be generated), when migrations are pending, and when a .sql file...
- `defineConfig` (function): Validates and normalizes a migration config (pure).
- `denoMigrationFileSystem` (function): Default {@link MigrationFileSystem} backed by Deno file APIs.
- `DiscoveredMigration` (interface): A migration discovered on disk by {@link readMigrationsDir}.
- `DriftCheckInput` (interface): Inputs to {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftFinding` (interface): A single drift finding from {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftKind` (typeAlias): Drift categories reported by {@link checkDrift}.
- `DriftReport` (interface): Result of a drift check.
- `GeneratedMigrationFile` (interface): A generated migration ready to be written to disk.
- `MigrateConfig` (interface): Validated migration configuration (sisal.migrate.ts).
- `MigrateProvider` (typeAlias): A migration runtime provider that refines a dialect's default adapter.
- `MigrationFileSystem` (interface): Minimal filesystem surface used by the migration workflow.
- `nextMigrationSequence` (function): Returns the next sequence number after the highest discovered migration.
- `parseMigrationSequence` (function): Extracts the leading numeric sequence from a migration id (0002_x → 2).
- `readMigrationsDir` (function): Reads and orders the .sql migrations in a directory (with their snapshots).
- `writeMigrationFile` (function): Writes a generated migration's .sql and .snapshot.json files.

### @sisal/pg . (packages/pg/mod.ts)

PostgreSQL adapter package for Sisal.

- `connect` (function): Convenience alias for {@link createPgDb} — opens a Postgres-backed database.
- `createPgDb` (function): Opens a PostgreSQL-backed database facade.
- `CreatePgDbOptions` (interface): Options for opening a PostgreSQL-backed database facade.
- `createPgExecutor` (function): Creates a PostgreSQL SQL executor from an existing executor, pool, client, or URL.
- `createPgMigrationDriver` (function): Creates a core migration driver backed by PostgreSQL SQL execution.
- `createPgMigrationHistoryStore` (function): Creates a migration history store persisted in a PostgreSQL table.
- `createPgMigrator` (function): Creates a PostgreSQL migration facade with a database-backed history store.
- `createPgOrmDriver` (function): Creates an ORM driver backed by a PostgreSQL executor, pool, client, or URL.
- `createPgPool` (function): Creates a PostgreSQL pool using the bundled @db/postgres driver.
- `DEFAULT_PG_MIGRATION_TABLE` (variable): Default PostgreSQL table used to store applied migration history.
- `generatePostgresAddColumn` (function): Generates an ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statement.
- `generatePostgresColumnDefinition` (function): Renders a single column definition for CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN.
- `generatePostgresColumnType` (function): Maps a snapshot column type to a PostgreSQL type expression.
- `generatePostgresCreateTable` (function): Generates a CREATE TABLE statement for one snapshot table.
- `generatePostgresUpStatements` (function): Generates the **non-destructive** PostgreSQL up statements for migrating from → to: CREATE TABLE for new tables and ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for new columns. Destructive changes (drop table/column, column type changes)...
- `PgClient` (interface): Minimal PostgreSQL client surface used by the ORM adapter.
- `PgConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options accepted by PostgreSQL ORM adapter factories.
- `PgDatabase` (interface): PostgreSQL-specialized database facade.
- `PgMigrateOptions` (interface): Options for applying pending PostgreSQL migrations.
- `PgMigrationDefinition` (interface): PostgreSQL convenience migration definition with inferred programmatic kind.
- `PgMigrationDriverOptions` (interface): Options for creating a PostgreSQL-backed migration driver.
- `PgMigrationHistoryStoreOptions` (interface): Options for creating a PostgreSQL-backed migration history store.
- `PgMigrationInput` (typeAlias): Migration input accepted by the PostgreSQL migrator facade.
- `PgMigrationPlanOptions` (interface): Options for planning PostgreSQL migrations without executing them.
- `PgMigrator` (interface): PostgreSQL migration facade backed by the core migrator.
- `PgOrmDriverOptions` (interface): Options for creating a PostgreSQL-backed ORM driver.
- `PgPool` (interface): Minimal PostgreSQL pool surface used by the ORM adapter.
- `pgQualifiedName` (function): Renders the qualified, quoted "schema"."table" name.
- `PgQueryResult` (interface): Rows and affected-row count returned by a PostgreSQL executor.
- `PgRollbackOptions` (interface): Options for rolling back PostgreSQL migrations.
- `PgSqlExecutor` (interface): Minimal SQL executor used by the PostgreSQL ORM adapter.
- `POSTGRES_DIALECT` (variable): SQL dialect name used by the PostgreSQL ORM adapter.
- `PostgresUpStatements` (interface): Safe (additive) up statements plus the destructive changes that were withheld.
- `quotePgIdent` (function): Quotes a PostgreSQL identifier, escaping embedded double quotes.

### @sisal/pg ./ddl (packages/pg/migrate/ddl.ts)

Pure PostgreSQL DDL generation from @sisal/orm snapshots.

- `generatePostgresAddColumn` (function): Generates an ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statement.
- `generatePostgresColumnDefinition` (function): Renders a single column definition for CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN.
- `generatePostgresColumnType` (function): Maps a snapshot column type to a PostgreSQL type expression.
- `generatePostgresCreateTable` (function): Generates a CREATE TABLE statement for one snapshot table.
- `generatePostgresForeignKeys` (function): Generates ALTER TABLE … ADD … FOREIGN KEY statements for a table. Foreign keys are emitted *after* every CREATE TABLE so the alphabetical table order in a snapshot never produces a forward-reference error on Postgres.
- `generatePostgresIndexes` (function): Generates CREATE [UNIQUE] INDEX statements for a table's indexes.
- `generatePostgresUpStatements` (function): Generates the **non-destructive** PostgreSQL up statements for migrating from → to: CREATE TABLE for new tables and ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for new columns. Destructive changes (drop table/column, column type changes)...
- `pgQualifiedName` (function): Renders the qualified, quoted "schema"."table" name.
- `PostgresUpStatements` (interface): Safe (additive) up statements plus the destructive changes that were withheld.
- `quotePgIdent` (function): Quotes a PostgreSQL identifier, escaping embedded double quotes.

- `PgMigrationPlanOptions` (interface): Options for planning PostgreSQL migrations without executing them.
- `PgMigrator` (interface): PostgreSQL migration facade backed by the core migrator.
- `PgPool` (interface): Minimal PostgreSQL pool surface used by the migration adapter.
- `pgQualifiedName` (function): Renders the qualified, quoted "schema"."table" name.
- `PgRollbackOptions` (interface): Options for rolling back PostgreSQL migrations.
- `PostgresUpStatements` (interface): Safe (additive) up statements plus the destructive changes that were withheld.
- `QueryResult` (interface): Rows and affected-row count returned by a PostgreSQL migration executor.
- `quotePgIdent` (function): Quotes a PostgreSQL identifier, escaping embedded double quotes.
- `SqlExecutor` (interface): Minimal SQL executor used by the PostgreSQL migration adapter.
- `SqlExecutorSession` (interface): Pinned PostgreSQL execution session held across multiple executor calls.

### @sisal/sqlite . (packages/sqlite/mod.ts)

SQLite adapter package for Sisal.

- `connect` (function): Convenience alias for {@link createSqliteDb} — opens a SQLite-backed database.
- `createSqliteDb` (function): Opens a SQLite-backed database facade.
- `CreateSqliteDbOptions` (interface): Options for opening a SQLite-backed database facade.
- `createSqliteExecutor` (function): Creates a SQLite SQL executor from an existing executor or open database.
- `createSqliteMigrationDriver` (function): Creates a core migration driver backed by SQLite SQL execution.
- `createSqliteMigrationHistoryStore` (function): Creates a migration history store persisted in a SQLite table.
- `createSqliteMigrator` (function): Creates a SQLite migration facade with a database-backed history store.
- `CreateSqliteMigratorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a SQLite migration facade.
- `createSqliteOrmDriver` (function): Creates an ORM driver backed by a SQLite executor or open database.
- `DEFAULT_SQLITE_MIGRATION_TABLE` (variable): Default SQLite table used to store applied migration history.
- `generateSqliteAddColumn` (function): Generates an ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statement.
- `generateSqliteColumnDefinition` (function): Renders a single column definition for CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN.
- `generateSqliteColumnType` (function): Maps a snapshot column type to a SQLite type/affinity. SQLite is dynamically typed with five affinities (TEXT/INTEGER/REAL/NUMERIC/BLOB); higher level types map onto the closest one (dates/JSON/UUIDs → TEXT, booleans...
- `generateSqliteCreateTable` (function): Generates a CREATE TABLE statement for one snapshot table.
- `generateSqliteUpStatements` (function): Generates the **non-destructive** SQLite up statements for migrating from → to: CREATE TABLE for new tables and ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for new columns. Destructive changes (drop table/column, column type changes) are...
- `openSqliteDatabase` (function): Opens a SQLite database with the bundled @db/sqlite driver. The driver is imported lazily (a dynamic import), so merely importing @sisal/orm/sqlite — and running the package's fake-backed tests — needs no...
- `quoteSqliteIdent` (function): Quotes a SQLite identifier, escaping embedded double quotes.
- `SQLITE_DIALECT` (variable): SQL dialect name used by the SQLite ORM adapter.
- `sqliteColumnAffinity` (function): SQLite storage-class affinity for a Sisal {@link ColumnDataType}.
- `SqliteConnectionOptions` (interface): Options for opening a SQLite database.
- `SqliteDatabase` (interface): SQLite-specialized database facade.
- `SqliteExecutorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a SQLite SQL executor.
- `SqliteLikeDatabase` (interface): Minimal SQLite database surface used by the ORM adapter. The bundled @db/sqlite Database satisfies it structurally, and tests inject a fake.
- `SqliteMigrateOptions` (interface): Options for applying pending SQLite migrations.
- `SqliteMigrationDefinition` (interface): SQLite convenience migration definition with inferred programmatic kind.
- `SqliteMigrationDriverOptions` (typeAlias): Options for creating a SQLite-backed migration driver.
- `SqliteMigrationHistoryStoreOptions` (interface): Options for creating a SQLite-backed migration history store.
- `SqliteMigrationInput` (typeAlias): Migration input accepted by the SQLite migrator facade.
- `SqliteMigrationPlanOptions` (interface): Options for planning SQLite migrations without executing them.
- `SqliteMigrator` (interface): SQLite migration facade backed by the core migrator.
- `SqliteOrmDriverOptions` (typeAlias): Options for creating a SQLite-backed ORM driver.
- `SqliteQueryResult` (interface): Rows and affected-row count returned by a SQLite executor.
- `SqliteRollbackOptions` (interface): Options for rolling back SQLite migrations.
- `SqliteSqlExecutor` (interface): Minimal SQL executor used by the SQLite ORM adapter.
- `SqliteStatement` (interface): A prepared statement: all returns rows, run returns the change count.
- `SqliteUpStatements` (interface): Safe (additive) up statements plus the destructive changes that were withheld.
- `statementReturnsRows` (function): Returns true when a statement yields rows (SELECT/WITH/PRAGMA/EXPLAIN, or any statement with a RETURNING clause), so the executor knows whether to read rows or report a change count.

- `openSqliteDatabase` (function): Opens a SQLite database with the bundled @db/sqlite driver. The driver is imported lazily (a dynamic import), so merely importing @sisal/orm/sqlite — and running the package's fake-backed tests — needs no...
- `SQLITE_DIALECT` (variable): SQL dialect name used by the SQLite ORM adapter.
